content.thinking:
Good, there's already an `es-http-tls` secret in the elasticsearch namespace. This likely contains the TLS certificate for Elasticsearch. However, for the ingress, I need a TLS secret in the ingress-nginx namespace or I can reference it from the elasticsearch namespace, but ingress resources reference secrets in their own namespace.

Let me look at the TLS secret to understand what we have.

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-cluster-0
      - es-cluster-1
      - es-cluster-2

    node.store.allow_mmap: false

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.autoconfiguration.enabled: false

    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.verification_mode: certificate

    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- Elasticsearch is running with HTTPS enabled (xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true)
- The `es-http` service is a ClusterIP on port 9200
- The `es-http-tls` secret contains `ca.crt`, `tls.crt`, `tls.key`
- Ingress-nginx is installed with the `nginx` ingress class

To make the API reachable at `https://es.example.com` via ingress-nginx, I need to:

1. Create a TLS secret in the `elasticsearch` namespace (or copy the existing one) for the ingress to use for TLS termination at the ingress level
2. Place the Ingress resource in the `elasticsearch` namespace alongside the backend service, and configure it to proxy HTTPS traffic to the Elasticsearch backend since it's already using SSL

. I need to decide whether to reuse the existing `es-http-tls` secret or generate a new certificate specifically for `es.example.com`, since the current cert may not include that domain as a SAN. Let me verify what the existing certificate actually covers.

content.text:
Elasticsearch uses HTTPS (TLS enabled at the ES level). I need to:
1. Create a TLS cert for `es.example.com` for the ingress
2. Create an Ingress with HTTPS-only enforcement and backend-protocol HTTPS

Let me check the existing cert's SANs and then create a new cert for `es.example.com`.

input.command: kubectl get secret es-http-tls -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -text 2>&1 | grep -A 5 "Subject Alternative\|Subject:" | head -20
input.description: Check SANs on existing ES TLS cert

content.content:
Subject: CN = es-http
        Subject Public Key Info:
            Public Key Algorithm: rsaEncryption
                Public-Key: (2048 bit)
                Modulus:
                    00:a3:37:d2:13:b7:61:5d:bc:a5:09:56:4c:3e:de:
--
            X509v3 Subject Alternative Name: 
                DNS:localhost, DNS:es-http, DNS:es-http.elasticsearch, DNS:es-http.elasticsearch.svc, DNS:es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local, DNS:*.elasticsearch.svc, DNS:*.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
            X509v3 Subject Key Identifier: 
                08:34:7D:46:8E:96:2F:EE:EC:50:30:8E:9C:4A:22:96:45:28:04:34
            X509v3 Authority Key Identifier: 
                55:25:A4:00:2C:60:E6:28:87:AC:56:D6:59:20:5F:93:A4:60:25:39

content.thinking:
The existing `es-http-tls` certificate doesn't cover `es.example.com`. I need to create a new TLS certificate for the ingress that covers `es.example.com`.

Since there's an `openssl-toolbox` pod running, I can use it to generate a self-signed certificate for `es.example.com`.

Let me create a self-signed certificate for `es.example.com` and then create an ingress with:
1. TLS secret for `es.example.com`
2. Backend protocol HTTPS (since ES uses HTTPS)
3. SSL redirect (to ensure plain HTTP doesn't work)
4. The `nginx` ingress class

I'll generate the certificate using the openssl-toolbox pod via kubectl exec.

content.text: The existing cert doesn't cover `es.example.com`. I'll generate a self-signed cert for it, then create the Ingress.

input.command:
kubectl exec openssl-toolbox -n elasticsearch -- openssl req -x509 -nodes -newkey rsa:2048 \
  -keyout /tmp/tls.key \
  -out /tmp/tls.crt \
  -days 365 \
  -subj "/CN=es.example.com" \
  -addext "subjectAltName=DNS:es.example.com" 2>&1
input.description: Generate self-signed cert for es.example.com in toolbox pod

input.command:
TLS_CRT=$(kubectl exec openssl-toolbox -n elasticsearch -- cat /tmp/tls.crt | base64 -w 0)
TLS_KEY=$(kubectl exec openssl-toolbox -n elasticsearch -- cat /tmp/tls.key | base64 -w 0)
echo "TLS_CRT length: ${#TLS_CRT}"
echo "TLS_KEY length: ${#TLS_KEY}"
input.description: Read the generated cert and key from toolbox pod

description: Read the generated cert and key from toolbox pod

summary: Read the generated cert and key from toolbox pod

input.command:
kubectl exec openssl-toolbox -n elasticsearch -- cat /tmp/tls.crt | kubectl create secret tls es-example-com-tls \
  --cert=/dev/stdin \
  --key=/dev/stdin \
  -n elasticsearch 2>&1 || true

# Use a different approach - get files from pod
kubectl exec openssl-toolbox -n elasticsearch -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/tls.crt' > /tmp/es-example-tls.crt
kubectl exec openssl-toolbox -n elasticsearch -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/tls.key' > /tmp/es-example-tls.key
kubectl create secret tls es-example-com-tls \
  --cert=/tmp/es-example-tls.crt \
  --key=/tmp/es-example-tls.key \
  -n elasticsearch 2>&1
input.description: Create TLS secret for es.example.com in elasticsearch namespace

description: Create TLS secret for es.example.com in elasticsearch namespace

content.text:
Good, the TLS secret is created. Now I'll create the Ingress resource that:
- Uses the `nginx` ingress class
- Routes `es.example.com` to the `es-http` service on port 9200
- Terminates TLS (HTTPS in, HTTPS out to backend)
- Redirects plain HTTP to HTTPS

input.content:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: es-ingress
  namespace: elasticsearch
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: "HTTPS"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/force-ssl-redirect: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-ssl-verify: "off"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - es.example.com
    secretName: es-example-com-tls
  rules:
  - host: es.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: es-http
            port:
              number: 9200
